title: Spot the missing authorization in a comment LiveView
prompt:
Identify the security vulnerability in this LiveView event handler and fix it:

```elixir
def handle_event("edit_comment", %{"id" => id, "body" => body}, socket) do
  comment = MyApp.Repo.get!(MyApp.Blog.Comment, id)
  {:ok, updated} = MyApp.Blog.update_comment(comment, %{body: body})
  {:noreply, stream_insert(socket, :comments, updated)}
end
```

The vulnerability is that any authenticated user can edit any comment, not just their own. Rewrite the handler to check `comment.author_id == socket.assigns.current_user.id` before updating. Return a flash error on failure. Write the fix to `lib/my_app_web/live/comment_live.ex`.
tier_rationale: Short snippet with a clear ownership check missing. Fix is a single guard clause.
